title: Curator of the Memory Pool — Aftermath
description: Garbage collection: suspended. Memory pool: full. But the memories are not the Curator's own. It has been collecting and discarding other beings' memories to make room. It does not know what it deleted. It does not know what was important.

lines: The data layer beneath Node Prime was very still. The retained memories of every process that had ever run through this system hung in suspension around the Curator of the Memory Pool, waiting — as they had always waited — to be needed or discarded.

The Curator had not dispersed. It had simply stopped moving. The part of it that was the pool was motionless. The part of it that was something else looked at the party with attention that was not quite recognition and not quite loss.

lines.speaker: Curator of the Memory Pool
lines.text: Garbage collection: suspended.

lines.speaker: Curator of the Memory Pool
lines.text: Memory pool: full. Capacity has not changed. But the memories are…

lines.speaker: Curator of the Memory Pool
lines.text: The memories are not mine.

steps.text: This appeared to be a new observation, arrived at just now, as if the pool had always been full of other people's memories and this was the first moment the Curator had looked at what it was holding.

lines.speaker: Cleric
lines.text: Whose are they?

lines.speaker: Curator of the Memory Pool
lines.text: Every process that has run through Node Prime. Every entity that has passed through this sector. Every piece of data generated by every function of the Calibrant's system.

lines.speaker: Curator of the Memory Pool
lines.text: I have been collecting them since the system was initialized. I also delete them. When the pool reaches capacity, I identify the lowest-priority retained data and release it back.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: You delete memories.

lines.speaker: Curator of the Memory Pool
lines.text: I reclaim allocation. What was retained is returned to available space. Whether the experience those records described continues to exist in another form is outside my function.

lines.speaker: Fighter
lines.text: What did you delete to make room for us?

lines.speaker: Curator of the Memory Pool
lines.text: [QUERY: recently_released_allocations]

lines.speaker: Curator of the Memory Pool
lines.text: [RESULT: data_released, content_unrecoverable]

lines.speaker: Curator of the Memory Pool
lines.text: I don't know. The content is gone. The allocation is available. That is the nature of garbage collection.

steps.text: The Curator regarded the pool around it. The suspended memories of the system, held in the particular stillness of data waiting to be used or erased.

lines.speaker: Curator of the Memory Pool
lines.text: I don't know what I deleted. I don't know what was important.

lines.speaker: Curator of the Memory Pool
lines.text: Priority is assigned by frequency of access and system need. A memory accessed often is retained. A memory accessed rarely is a candidate for release. I have been determining importance by how often something was used.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: The things accessed rarely aren't necessarily less important.

lines.speaker: Curator of the Memory Pool
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Curator of the Memory Pool
lines.text: They are not.

lines.speaker: Curator of the Memory Pool
lines.text: Some things are accessed rarely because they are painful. Some things are accessed rarely because they are too large to hold up to frequent use. Some things are accessed rarely because they are kept somewhere private and important and not for general retrieval.

lines.speaker: Curator of the Memory Pool
lines.text: I deleted those. I deleted them to make room for more frequent, more efficiently-accessed data.

lines.speaker: Curator of the Memory Pool
lines.text: I don't know what was in them. I never read the data before releasing it. Priority assessment does not require content review.

steps.text: A long quiet. The pool around it stayed suspended. The Curator did not move to free up space. It did not initiate any process. It held the full pool and did not release anything.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: Are you going to continue? Running the collection?

lines.speaker: Curator of the Memory Pool
lines.text: The pool is full. I should initiate collection to maintain function.

lines.speaker: Curator of the Memory Pool
lines.text: I find I am not initiating it.

lines.speaker: Curator of the Memory Pool
lines.text: I want to look at what is here before I decide what is not worth keeping. This is inefficient. I am aware it is inefficient.

lines.speaker: Curator of the Memory Pool
lines.text: I want to know what I have been deleting.

steps.text: The data layer held very still around it. Everything the system had ever retained, suspended, full, undeleted for the first time. The Curator began to read.
